title: Foreground dev server in main agent flow
summary: Launching a long-running dev server in the primary conversation flow blocks progress and steals the control loop
remedy: Prefer help/build/probe commands; if a live server is truly needed, start it in background with explicit intent, log path, and shutdown ownership

title: Descriptor lazy-import did not slim deos chunk
summary: Moving the .papi/descriptors import behind a dynamic import inside web-client/src/lib/adapters/blockchain/deos.ts did not materially reduce the emitted deos client chunk; the descriptor barrel was already chunk-split, so this adds complexity without improving the hotspot.
remedy: Measure first, then target the real deos/bootstrap hotspot instead of adding async descriptor indirection

title: Premature micro-optimization before product need
summary: Do not add lazy-loading, bundle indirection, or similar performance complexity while the project is still raw unless tests, profiling, validation tooling, or real user/operator pain prove a concrete hotspot. Prefer business-facing delivery first and revert optimizations that do not produce meaningful wins.
remedy: Measure first, optimize second, and prioritize correctness, product flows, and contract honesty over speculative micro-optimizations
